_catalog.name: CargoTrack HS Code Intelligence Catalog
sources: BIS Commerce Control List (CCL) — Export Administration Regulations
_catalog.note: HS Chapter classifications cover the top 50 chapters by global trade volume. 4-digit heading detail is provided for high-risk or strategically significant headings.

chapters.label: Live Animals
chapters.customsNotes: CITES permits required for endangered species (Appendix I, II). Phytosanitary/veterinary health certificates required. Import restrictions in many countries.

chapters.label: Meat and Edible Meat Offal
chapters.customsNotes: Food safety certification mandatory. Halal/Kosher certification where required. Cold chain documentation critical. Country-of-origin labelling required in most markets.

chapters.label: Fish and Crustaceans, Molluscs
chapters.customsNotes: Catch certificates required (EU IUU Regulation). Aquaculture products need health certificate. CITES for some shark fins, seahorses. Halal certification for Muslim markets.
chapters.antiDumpingNotes: EU and US anti-dumping duties on shrimp from certain Asian countries

chapters.label: Edible Fruit and Nuts
chapters.customsNotes: Phytosanitary certificate mandatory. Pesticide residue compliance (MRL). Country-of-origin labelling. Some countries restrict import from pest-affected regions.

chapters.label: Coffee, Tea, Maté and Spices
chapters.customsNotes: Phytosanitary certificate. Food safety certification. Geographic Indications (GI) — e.g., Darjeeling tea, Ceylon tea — may be protected. ICO coffee export certification for some markets.

chapters.label: Cereals
chapters.customsNotes: Phytosanitary certificate. GMO labelling/authorization requirements. Some countries require non-GMO certificate. TRQs (Tariff Rate Quotas) common for cereals.

chapters.label: Oil Seeds and Oleaginous Fruits
chapters.customsNotes: Phytosanitary certificate. Deforestation regulations (EU EUDR for palm oil, soya). GMO compliance required. Moisture content documentation for risk of spontaneous combustion in large quantities.
keyComplianceConcerns: Spontaneous combustion risk in bulk (Class 4.2 DG)

chapters.label: Animal/Vegetable Fats and Oils
chapters.customsNotes: EUDR applies to palm oil. Halal certification required for many markets. Sustainability certification (RSPO for palm oil). Saponification value tests for customs classification.

chapters.label: Beverages, Spirits and Vinegar
chapters.dgNotes: High-alcohol beverages (>24% ABV) classified as Class 3 Flammable Liquid for air freight
chapters.customsNotes: Alcohol import prohibited or strictly licensed in Islamic-majority countries (Saudi Arabia, UAE for non-free zones, Indonesia). Age verification. Geographic Indications (Champagne, Cognac, Scotch Whisky, Bourbon). Excise duties significant.

chapters.label: Tobacco and Manufactured Tobacco Substitutes
chapters.customsNotes: High excise duty globally. WHO FCTC compliance. Track and trace requirements (EU TPD, US FDA). Many countries require import permit. Tight regulatory environment.

chapters.label: Mineral Fuels, Mineral Oils, Petroleum Products
chapters.dgNotes: Most petroleum products are Class 3 Flammable Liquids. LPG and natural gas are Class 2.
chapters.customsNotes: Oil price cap sanctions on Russia (G7): crude ≤$60/barrel, petroleum products ≤$45-100/barrel depending on type. Price attestation required. OFAC compliance for oil from sanctioned origins.

criticalHeadings.2709: Crude petroleum — Russian origin subject to oil price cap
criticalHeadings.2710: Petroleum products (diesel, petrol, jet fuel) — most common Class 3 DG

chapters.label: Inorganic Chemicals (acids, alkalis, salts, metals)
chapters.dgNotes: This chapter contains multiple DG classes: compressed gases, oxidizers, toxic substances, radioactive isotopes, corrosives
chapters.customsNotes: Broad chapter with many dual-use chemicals. Export license required for many chemicals under EU Dual-Use Reg 2021/821 and U.S. EAR CCL. Chemical Weapons Convention (CWC) schedules apply to some headings.
chapters.antiDumpingNotes: EU AD duties on sodium gluconate, citric acid, other inorganics from China

chapters.label: Organic Chemicals
chapters.dgNotes: Organic chemicals span multiple DG classes: flammable liquids (solvents), toxic substances, corrosives
chapters.customsNotes: Chemical precursor controls. EU Dual-Use Regulation and U.S. EAR CCL cover many organic chemicals. Drug precursor controls (DEA in US, INCB internationally). CWC Schedule 2/3 chemicals include many common organic compounds.
chapters.antiDumpingNotes: EU AD on many Chinese organic chemicals

criticalHeadings.2921: Amine-function compounds — industrial amines (CWC Schedule 2 for some)
criticalHeadings.2933: Heterocyclic compounds — includes some pharmaceutical precursors

chapters.label: Pharmaceutical Products
chapters.customsNotes: Import licensing mandatory in most countries. GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) certificates required. Controlled substances (narcotics, psychotropics) require INCB import/export authorization. Cold chain for biologics. Market Authorization required.

criticalHeadings.3002: Vaccines, blood, toxins, cultures — Class 6.2 infectious substances if applicable

chapters.label: Fertilizers
chapters.dgNotes: Ammonium nitrate fertilizers are Class 5.1 Oxidizing substances and dual-use explosive precursors
chapters.customsNotes: Ammonium nitrate: dual-use — fertilizer and explosive precursor. Many countries require licenses for import/export. CBAM applies in EU (carbon content). Russian fertilizers sanctioned/restricted.
chapters.antiDumpingNotes: US and EU anti-dumping on fertilizers from Russia, China, and other origins

criticalHeadings.3102: Ammonium nitrate — explosive precursor, Class 5.1, end-use critical

chapters.label: Tanning/Dyeing Extracts, Paints, Inks
chapters.dgNotes: Most paints, varnishes, lacquers contain flammable solvents — Class 3
chapters.customsNotes: VOC content limits apply in EU and US for environmental compliance. REACH registration for chemical components. Anti-dumping on Chinese titanium dioxide.
chapters.antiDumpingNotes: EU anti-dumping on titanium dioxide from China; pigments subject to scrutiny

chapters.label: Essential Oils, Perfumes, Cosmetics
chapters.dgNotes: Perfumes and many essential oils are Class 3 Flammable Liquids (alcohol content)
chapters.customsNotes: Cosmetics registration required in many markets (EU CPNP, China NMPA, India licensing). Alcohol-based products classified as DG. Ingredient labelling requirements. IFRA standards for fragrance.

chapters.label: Explosives, Pyrotechnic Products, Matches
chapters.dgNotes: All products in this chapter are Class 1 Explosives — most FORBIDDEN by air
chapters.customsNotes: Government license required in virtually every country for import and export. High diversion risk. End-user certificate mandatory. UN Number and proper shipping name on all packaging. Cannot transit without authorization.

criticalHeadings.3604: Fireworks, signal flares, rockets — Class 1 DG

chapters.label: Miscellaneous Chemical Products
chapters.dgNotes: Mixed chapter — industrial chemicals span multiple DG classes
chapters.customsNotes: REACH pre-registration required for EU. Biocidal Products Regulation (EU) for pest control. Some headings include dual-use chemicals. Anti-dumping duties on biodiesel from various origins.
chapters.antiDumpingNotes: EU and US anti-dumping duties on biodiesel from Argentina, Indonesia

chapters.label: Plastics and Articles Thereof
chapters.customsNotes: EU Single Use Plastics Directive restrictions. Plastic waste trade restrictions (Basel Convention amendment). Some plastic raw materials may be DG (e.g., reactive monomers). Anti-dumping on Chinese polyester film, PET.
chapters.antiDumpingNotes: EU/US anti-dumping on PET, polyester film, certain plastics from China

chapters.label: Rubber and Articles Thereof
chapters.customsNotes: Natural rubber: phytosanitary requirements. Used tyres trade heavily restricted (environmental). Deforestation regulations (EUDR) apply to natural rubber as of 2025.
chapters.antiDumpingNotes: Anti-dumping on Chinese tyres in US and EU

chapters.label: Wood and Articles of Wood
chapters.customsNotes: EUDR (EU Deforestation Regulation): timber importers must prove no deforestation. U.S. Lacey Act: import of illegally harvested wood prohibited. CITES for protected timber species (rosewood, ebony). Phytosanitary certificate for wood packaging (ISPM 15).
chapters.antiDumpingNotes: US AD/CVD on Chinese wood flooring, plywood

chapters.label: Paper and Paperboard
chapters.customsNotes: Waste paper: Basel Convention amendment (from 2021, trade between OECD and non-OECD requires prior consent). Anti-dumping on coated paper from China and Indonesia.
chapters.antiDumpingNotes: EU/US anti-dumping on coated fine paper from China, Indonesia

chapters.label: Silk
chapters.customsNotes: Certificate of origin important for tariff preference claims. Forced labour risk — Xinjiang cotton/textiles supply chain due diligence.
keyComplianceConcerns: Supply chain forced labour due diligence (Xinjiang)

chapters.label: Cotton
chapters.customsNotes: UFLPA (Uyghur Forced Labor Prevention Act — US): rebuttable presumption that goods from Xinjiang are made with forced labour. VERY HIGH scrutiny on Chinese cotton. Importer must prove non-Xinjiang origin or use non-China origin cotton.
requiredDocumentsImport: Supply Chain Due Diligence Documentation (Xinjiang risk)
chapters.antiDumpingNotes: US anti-dumping on Chinese cotton products
keyComplianceConcerns: UFLPA forced labour (Xinjiang cotton — critical risk)

chapters.label: Knitted or Crocheted Clothing
chapters.customsNotes: UFLPA concerns for China-origin clothing. Rules of Origin particularly complex — 'substantial transformation' doctrine. Country of origin marking required on all garments (US). Flammability standards compliance.

chapters.label: Woven Clothing and Apparel
chapters.customsNotes: Same considerations as Chapter 61. High tariff rates on China-origin garments in US (Section 301). Rules of Origin: yarn-forward rule applies under most US FTAs.

chapters.label: Other Textile Articles
chapters.customsNotes: Similar to Chapters 61-62 for forced labour and origin concerns. Used textiles/clothing: regulated under EU textile waste regulations. Some countries ban used clothing imports.

chapters.label: Precious Metals, Stones, Jewellery, Coins
chapters.customsNotes: Kimberley Process Certification Scheme (KPCS) mandatory for rough diamonds. Conflict minerals reporting (U.S. Dodd-Frank Section 1502). Russia: G7 banned Russian diamonds >1 carat. High value — CVD risk for gold from conflict zones. Declared value accuracy critical.

criticalHeadings.7102: Diamonds — Kimberley Process mandatory, Russia ban

chapters.label: Iron and Steel
chapters.customsNotes: Section 232 tariffs: 25% steel, 10% aluminium (US). EU CBAM: carbon pricing applies from 2026. Russia sanctions: most steel banned in US and EU. Anti-dumping extensive on Chinese, Indian, and other origins. Mill certificates required.
chapters.antiDumpingNotes: Extensive AD/CVD orders on steel from China, India, Korea, Taiwan, Russia (US and EU)

chapters.label: Articles of Iron or Steel
chapters.customsNotes: Anti-dumping duties on many Chinese and other origin steel articles. Section 232 tariffs may apply based on steel content. Mill certificates for high-specification items.
chapters.antiDumpingNotes: AD on welded tubes, wire rods, wire hangers, etc. from China

chapters.label: Aluminium and Articles Thereof
chapters.customsNotes: Section 232: 10% tariff on aluminium (US). CBAM applies from 2026 (EU). Russia sanctions: Russian aluminium restricted (RUSAL-connected entities). Anti-dumping on Chinese aluminium extrusions.
chapters.antiDumpingNotes: AD on Chinese aluminium extrusions (US and EU)

chapters.label: Nuclear Reactors, Boilers, Machinery and Mechanical Appliances
chapters.customsNotes: Broad chapter covering significant dual-use goods. BIS CCL covers many headings under ECCNs 2A, 2B, 3B, 4A. Semiconductor manufacturing equipment: heightened controls (EAR/EU). Machine tools: controlled destinations. CE marking (EU) mandatory.
chapters.antiDumpingNotes: AD on Chinese machine tools, hydraulic systems, solar components

criticalHeadings.8401: Nuclear reactors — CRITICAL dual-use, NRC license required
criticalHeadings.8486: Semiconductor manufacturing equipment — ECCN 3B001/3B002, China license likely required

chapters.label: Electrical Machinery, Electronics, Computers
chapters.dgNotes: Lithium batteries in electronics (UN3481). Standalone lithium batteries (UN3480) also Class 9.
chapters.customsNotes: Very broad chapter. Major dual-use concerns: semiconductors (ECCN 3A001), encryption technology (ECCN 5E002), telecommunications (ECCN 5A001). Huawei and ZTE: Entity List — no supply without BIS license. Russia/China/Iran restrictions on electronics.
chapters.antiDumpingNotes: AD on Chinese solar panels, LED displays, large power transformers
keyComplianceConcerns: Huawei/ZTE Entity List — no supply without license

criticalHeadings.8471: Computers — ECCN 4A003 for high-performance; Xinjiang DAS lists
criticalHeadings.8473: Computer parts — verify ECCN for high-performance components
criticalHeadings.8542: Integrated circuits/semiconductors — ECCN 3A001 critical; China restrictions

chapters.label: Railway Locomotives, Rolling Stock
chapters.customsNotes: Large/heavy lift logistics required. Type approval certificates. Russia sanctions: trains/parts restricted.

chapters.label: Vehicles (Automobiles, Motorcycles, Parts)
chapters.dgNotes: Vehicles with fuel: Class 3 (flammable liquid in tank). EVs: Class 9 lithium batteries. Airbag inflators: Class 1.
chapters.customsNotes: Type approval mandatory (EU WVTA, US FMVSS). Safety standards compliance. Russia sanctions: vehicles restricted. Anti-dumping on Chinese EVs (EU 2024: up to 38% AD duties). CBP enforcement on VIN numbers.
chapters.antiDumpingNotes: EU: significant AD duties on Chinese EVs announced 2024 (BYD 17%, SAIC 38%). US: 100% tariff on Chinese EVs.
keyComplianceConcerns: EU anti-dumping on Chinese EVs (CRITICAL if EV)

chapters.label: Aircraft, Spacecraft
chapters.customsNotes: Export license from Dept of State (ITAR) mandatory for most aircraft and parts. EAR controls on commercial aircraft. Russia sanctions: no civilian aircraft or parts. ITAR controls virtually all military and many dual-use aerospace items.
keyComplianceConcerns: ITAR mandatory for virtually all aerospace

criticalHeadings.8802: Aircraft — ITAR/EAR license mandatory; Russia ban
criticalHeadings.8803: Aircraft parts — ITAR controls; Russia ban

chapters.label: Ships, Boats
chapters.customsNotes: ITAR controls on military vessels and dual-use maritime equipment. Russia sanctions: ships and components restricted. Flag of Convenience (FOC) vessels require careful OFAC screening. Dark fleet vessels used for sanctions evasion.

chapters.label: Optical, Photographic, Medical/Surgical Instruments
chapters.customsNotes: Medical devices: FDA 510(k)/PMA (US), CE marking (EU), CDSCO approval (India). Optical and precision instruments: dual-use ECCN controls. Radiation-emitting devices (X-ray): licensing in many countries. Biometric surveillance tech: export controls on human rights grounds (China).
chapters.antiDumpingNotes: EU/US AD on Chinese optical fibres

chapters.label: Arms, Ammunition, Parts
chapters.dgNotes: Ammunition and explosives are Class 1. Some weapons components may also be Class 1.
chapters.customsNotes: ITAR (State Dept — Munitions List) controls virtually all items in this chapter. Foreign Military Sales (FMS) or Direct Commercial Sales (DCS) license mandatory. UN arms embargo applies to many sanctioned countries. End-user certificate essential.
requiredDocumentsImport: ITAR License (State Dept) — DSP-5 or DSP-73

criticalHeadings.9302: Revolvers and pistols — strict licensing globally
criticalHeadings.9306: Bombs, grenades, missiles, cartridges — ITAR mandatory

chapters.label: Furniture, Bedding, Mattresses, Lighting
chapters.customsNotes: Flammability standards compliance (UK, EU, US CPSC). California Proposition 65 compliance (US). Anti-dumping extensive on Chinese wooden furniture. Timber content subject to EUDR/Lacey Act.
chapters.antiDumpingNotes: US AD/CVD on Chinese wooden bedroom furniture, dormitory furniture — very high duties

chapters.label: Toys, Games, Sports Equipment
chapters.customsNotes: Strict safety requirements: EU Toy Safety Directive, US CPSC/ASTM F963. CE marking mandatory (EU). Phthalate and chemical limits (REACH). Age labelling. Country-of-origin for US. BIS certification for India.

chapters.label: Semiconductor Manufacturing Equipment (Special Flag)
chapters.customsNotes: U.S. October 2022 and 2023 export control rules restrict export of advanced semiconductor manufacturing equipment to China. ECCN 3B001 (lithography), 3B002 (chip testing), 4B001 (computer-aided design tools for chips). Allied countries (Netherlands ASML, Japan Tokyo Electron) have adopted similar restrictions. No license to China for cutting-edge nodes (<16nm). End-user certificate and screening against Entity List mandatory.
keyComplianceConcerns: BIS October 2022/2023 China chip equipment controls — CRITICAL
